title: Nexteer puts steer-by-wire into series production
content: Steering systems specialist Nexteer Automotive says it has started series production of its steer-by-wire (SbW) system—its first SbW program in series production. The system launched on a production passenger vehicle for a leading Chinese new energy vehicle (NEV) manufacturer, helping to bring the world’s first production passenger vehicle with a full drive‑by‑wire chassis to market. Nexteer says the vehicle features Nexteer’s steer‑by‑wire (SbW) system as a key enabler. The SbW featured in this vehicle marks Nexteer’s first SbW system in series production, representing a ‘major step forward for the technology — moving from development and validation to full-scale production’. Certified in late 2025, this system achieved ASIL D functional safety approval from DAkkS (German Accreditation Body) through close collaboration with the OEM. This certification reflects global top-tier performance in fault diagnosis, redundancy, and safety monitoring, Nexteer says. According to Nexteer, key features include: Multi-layered redundancy design: Dual controllers, dual power supplies, multiple communication links, and dual actuation paths — achieving redundancy at system, hardware, and software levels. This ensures that in the event of a single fault, the backup path takes over within milliseconds with no loss of steering function. Full‑scenario functional safety mechanism: Multi‑level monitoring and fault handling strategies covering sensors, controllers, actuators, and communication links. Adjustable steering ratio: Automatically adjusts steering angle and effort based on vehicle speed and driving mode, balancing agility and comfort. Intuitive road‑feel simulation technology: Software‑defined steering feedback delivers a more responsive and precise driving experience, adaptable to a wide range of driving scenarios. Open interface for autonomous driving: As a key actuation layer for ADAS and autonomous driving systems, it provides real‑time, precise control capabilities, supporti

The system launched on a production passenger vehicle for a leading Chinese new energy vehicle (NEV) manufacturer, helping to bring the world’s first production passenger vehicle with a full drive‑by‑wire chassis to market. Nexteer says the vehicle features Nexteer’s steer‑by‑wire (SbW) system as a key enabler. The SbW featured in this vehicle marks Nexteer’s first SbW system in series production, representing a ‘major step forward for the technology — moving from development and validation to full-scale production’. Certified in late 2025, this system achieved ASIL D functional safety approval from DAkkS (German Accreditation Body) through close collaboration with the OEM. This certification reflects global top-tier performance in fault diagnosis, redundancy, and safety monitoring, Nexteer says. According to Nexteer, key features include: Multi-layered redundancy design: Dual controllers, dual power supplies, multiple communication links, and dual actuation paths — achieving redundancy at system, hardware, and software levels. This ensures that in the event of a single fault, the backup path takes over within milliseconds with no loss of steering function. summary_tr: Direksiyon sistemleri uzmanı Nexteer Automotive, direksiyon-tel-ile (steer-by-wire - SbW) sisteminin seri üretimine başladığını duyurdu. Bu sistem, Çinli önde gelen bir yeni enerji aracı (NEV) üreticisi için bir binek araçta kullanılarak dünyanın ilk tam tel-ile-sürüş şasili binek aracının pazara sunulmasına yardımcı oldu. Sistem, geliştirme ve doğrulama aşamasından tam ölçekli üretime geçerek teknoloji için önemli bir adım teşkil etmektedir ve ASIL D fonksiyonel güvenlik onayı almıştır.
reasoning: Nexteer'in gelişmiş steer-by-wire (SbW) sisteminin seri üretimine başlaması, şirketin teknolojik kapasitesinde ve pazar erişiminde önemli bir genişlemeyi temsil etmektedir. Bu, özellikle Çinli bir NEV üreticisi ile yapılan anlaşma ve ASIL D güvenlik sertifikası, şirketin inovasyonunu ve rekabet gücünü artırdığını göstermektedir.
